input: An 11-year-old boy with no significant medical history was admitted due to a 4-month history of headache, general condition decline, apathy, increased urination, constipation, swelling of feet and hands, and reduced muscle strength. Laboratory tests indicated mild anemia, iron deficiency, elevated eosinophil count, increased ESR, low vitamin D levels, elevated gamma globulin levels, high calcium levels, low phosphate levels, and increased urea and creatinine concentrations. A high calcium creatinine index and reduced parathormone levels were noted. MRI of the head showed no abnormalities. Abdominal ultrasound revealed thickening of the renal parenchymal layer and kidney enlargement. Thyroid and parathyroid ultrasound showed no abnormalities. The patient was treated with furosemide and infusion fluids without improvement. A subfebrile state was also noted. Further examinations included an abdominal ultrasound that continued to show kidney enlargement with a hyperechogenic signal of the renal parenchyma and liver enlargement. Whole-body MRI revealed hepatomegaly and a hyperintense signal in the kidneys and lower limb fascia. Bone scintigraphy and nailfold capillaroscopy showed no abnormalities. Bone marrow biopsy ruled out proliferative diseases.
